fields.description:
## Why
On failure the bot must NEVER land a partial/inconsistent stack and NEVER evict a change from within a stack (that would orphan its descendants). The whole stack returns to its owning agent to remediate ŌĆö this is the one Goal-2 exception (agent pulled in only when code must change).

## What
Handle the two failure modes in the auto-lander loop: CI-fail (post-rebase `Verified -1`) and rebase-conflict (`POST /rebase` `409`). The recorded outcome is the single source of truth consumed by the agent contract.

## Dependencies
Depends on **S2** (`perovskite-frilly-grebe`, 0ee8-08de-bf0f-45ec) ŌĆö the core loop: this story adds the failure/edge branches to S2's loop and consumes its `wipChain` + stack-selection, so the happy-path loop must exist first.

## Outcome record
The bot writes an **append-only JSON** record per failure to the auto-lander state dir (project-infra state, NOT a rebar event): `{stack_id, change_ids[], outcome Ōłł {needs_rebase, ci_failed}, failing_change_id?, tested_sha, recorded_at, acknowledged}`. It is **upsert-keyed on `stack_id`** (a re-run of the same stack overwrites its prior record) ŌĆö NOT one blind append per attempt. S4 (land command) and S9 (batch failure) consume this record as the authoritative outcome.

## Scope
Failure/edge paths of the core-loop service; the recorded outcome feeds the agent contract story.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] CI-fail: on a post-rebase `Verified -1`, record a `ci_failed` outcome, then remove `Autosubmit` from **all changes in the affected stack** (not just the failing change), and stop driving the stack; post NO bot comment (the native `-1` + run logs are the signal ŌĆö no redundant channel).
- [ ] Rebase-conflict: on `POST /rebase` `409`, record a `needs_rebase` outcome event-driven on that change/stack (NOT a continuously-maintained up-to-date vote ŌĆö that would churn O(open) per landing), then remove `Autosubmit` from all changes in the stack.
- [ ] **Stack ŌåÆ owner:** the owning agent is the stack's uploader/owner (`owner._account_id` on the tip change); "hand back" = record ONE outcome for the stack + remove `Autosubmit` from every change in it. Never partial-land; never evict a change from within a stack.
- [ ] Never `allow_conflicts` ŌĆö a `409` from `POST /rebase` routes to the `needs_rebase` path; no conflict-marker patchset is ever created or submitted.
- [ ] **Idempotent ordering under retries/restart:** the outcome is recorded **before** `Autosubmit` removal; label-removal treats `404`/`409` as a no-op; the `stack_id` upsert key makes re-runs overwrite rather than duplicate; on restart an unacknowledged record re-drives the label removal to completion.
